cells.value: Sizes the outsourced NEW-CONSTRUCTION supplier market for US Navy DDG-51 Flight III destroyers (SCN line item 2122).
Two building yards: GD Bath Iron Works (BIW, Maine) and HII Ingalls Shipbuilding (Mississippi). Market-entrant lens: Saronic.

TAM = non-GFE, non-MIB new-construction $ (Basic Construction + AP/LLTM/EOQ) tied to covered hulls, performed AWAY FROM prime / co-prime / prime-controlled / GFE-controlled sites - the dollars genuinely available to outside suppliers.
SAM = the portion of TAM in work-type buckets Saronic may target, shown as a scenario menu (not a probability-weighted haircut).
TAM = BC_base x BC_coeff  +  AP_LLTM_base x AP_LLTM_coeff   (one headline number; stream bridge underneath).
BC_base = DDG P-5c Basic Construction $ per FY, non-GFE/non-MIB, in-scope; allocated to BIW/Ingalls via the production schedule.
BC_coeff = $-weighted supplier+foreign POP share (other-US + foreign, away from the two yards) over gated, non-GFE, confirmed BC-stream actions - MYP-corrected (see below).
AP_LLTM_base = DDG P-10 advance procurement / EOQ, supplier-addressable (non-GFE) portion only. Editable Inputs placeholder (default 0) until the Phase-3 P-10<->P-5c reconciliation.
AP_LLTM_coeff = supplier+foreign POP share over AP/LLTM-stream actions.
Allocate TAM into 7 work-type buckets, then SAM_k = SUMPRODUCT(bucket_$, scenario_flags). No capability_fit, no addressability, no SOM-style haircut.

Bucket shares = observed subaward mix + EXPLICIT editable residual (not renormalized): the 7 buckets keep their observed share; unbucketed = 1 - sum(7 modeled) is shown and excluded from scenario SAM. So sum bucketed = TAM holds by construction.
DDG bucketing is DESCRIPTION-LED (classify on the award description_of_requirement); the submarine workbook is NAICS/vendor-led.
Leadership scenarios (1/0 columns): Metal components, HM&E components, Electrical/power, Modular assemblies, Broad component manufacturing.
The MYP correction - headline guardrail (NEVER present the 87%)
Two MYP master awards are POP-disclosed but DOLLAR-REDACTED: BIW N00024-23-C-2305 (~$6.40B) + Ingalls N00024-23-C-2307 (~$8.18B) = ~$14.58B.
The disclosed-only corpus reads ~87% outside-yards - a REDACTION ARTIFACT: it omits the two huge yard-heavy masters, over-weighting away-from-yard actions.

Folding the masters back at reconstructed POP weights (FPDS obligatedAmount from fpds_raw_v2/ + trade-press totals) corrects to ~33% outside-yards. The supplier coefficient is the non-GFE slice of that. Shown before/after on TAM (MYP + Coefficients sections).
ALWAYS travel the POP-coefficient numbers together: disclosed artifact -> MYP-corrected outside-yards POP -> applied BC supplier coefficient (the non-GFE slice). The deck is POP-only.
Excluded scope: GFE / weapons / sensors / ordnance / Navy-directed systems (Aegis, SPY-6, VLS, SM/ESSM/Tomahawk/CIWS); MIB/industrial-base; sustainment; depot; design-only.
DDG-specific dropped contaminants: IVECO Mk110 (M6785416C0006, ~$707M); DDG-1000/Zumwalt (LI 2119); WPN/OPN weapons; Thales ESSM (N0002415C5420, ~$4.2B NATO cost-share on a Raytheon PIID).
Entity / location are HINTS only - the award-action scope controls. Applied once, consistently: gated out of the POP corpus AND as bridge decrements; the Audit tab ties the two.
Audit-trail color key (numeric cells only; text / IDs stay black)
Blue (#0000FF) = hardcoded numeric input.   Black (#000000) = derived (formula result).   Green (#008000) = cross-sheet link to a numeric cell.
No yellow fills, no merged cells, no gridlines, no text wrap. Arial 8pt. Numbers #,##0.0; percentages italic 0.0%; zero renders as a dash.
SCN is single-vintage (FY27 President's Budget); HM&E is missing FY20-23, so BC_base is a lower bound pre-FY24.
FFATA first-tier subawards capture only ~15% of true subcontract flow (a coverage floor) - never present FFATA as the market.
FPDS prime feed de-capped via fpds_raw_v2/ (BIW 30,236 / Rolls-Royce 3,562 records) - required for prime-obligation accuracy.
POP is a proxy for where work is performed; the Audit tab risk-weights confirmation of high-$/redacted/MYP/AP rows before the coefficient is finalized.
Guide: Methodology (this tab - scope, definitions, exclusions, taxonomy, work-type rules).   Inputs: Control + Scenarios.
Model: TAM (normalized budget, coefficients, MYP, model), SAM (subawards, allocation, scenarios).
Outputs: Deck_Outputs.   Validation: Audit (gate, figures, sensitivity, scope, POP).   Sources: lineage + references.

2026-05-29: v5 rebuild started. DDG ported onto the shared workbook_core engine (em/en-dash normalize opt-in; submarine output proven byte-identical). The v4 '4-denominator / cost-funnel' destroyer workbook is superseded.

2026-05-31: consolidated 33 sheet modules -> 8 chapter files (SheetEntry); then merged the visible tabs into 10 composite chapters (this tab is the former README/Market_Definitions/Exclusion_Rules/Bucket_Taxonomy/Worktype_Rules).
TAM - the non-GFE, non-MIB new-construction supplier opportunity funded through Basic
Construction + AP/LLTM/EOQ (DDG-51 covered hulls), performed AWAY from prime / co-prime /
prime-controlled / GFE-controlled sites - the dollars genuinely available to outside suppliers.
SAM - the portion of TAM in work-type buckets Saronic may choose to target, shown as a
scenario menu (no capability-fit / addressability / win-probability haircut).
Headlines use the POP-based supplier TAM (BC_base x MYP-corrected BC coefficient + AP/LLTM stream),
with the budget bridge (SCN total ship cost -> Basic Construction base) and MYP correction as audit support.
P-5c BC + P-10 AP/LLTM/EOQ, net exclusions
BC_base x BC_coeff + AP_base x AP_coeff
Sum of bucket $ where scenario flag = 1
Two-stream TAM (v5): TAM = BC_base x BC_coeff + AP/LLTM_base x AP/LLTM_coeff
other-US + foreign, away from BIW + Ingalls yards; MYP masters folded back
(MYP correction: disclosed-only ~87% outside-yards is a redaction artifact -> ~33% corrected. AP/LLTM base is a placeholder pending Phase 3.)
Allocate TAM into 7 work-type buckets (description-led for DDG), then select per scenario:
SAM_scenario[k] = SUMPRODUCT(bucket_$, scenario_flag[k, bucket])   with flag in {0,1}
No capability_fit, no addressability, no SOM-style haircut - those are win-probability, not SAM.
"If leadership targets these buckets, the opportunity is $X."
GFE / Aegis / SPY-6 / VLS / weapons / ordnance
Governing principle - the award-action SCOPE controls (entity/location are hints only)
An award is EXCLUDED from TAM when its scope is GFE / weapons / sensors / ordnance /
Navy-directed systems procurement, MIB / industrial-base capacity, sustainment, depot, or
design-only - even if the work physically supports or installs on a covered DDG-51 hull.
Aegis / SPY-6 / VLS / weapons / ordnance / Navy-directed
DDG exclusion categories + named contaminants (see Audit -> Scope)
out of scope (closed; OPN modernization only)
Marine Corps gun; phantom DDG vendor
NATO cost-share on a Raytheon PIID; WPN-funded
Applied ONCE: gated out of the POP corpus AND decremented in the bridge; the Audit tab ties the two.

valve, pump, piping, pipe , fitting, manifold, hydraulic, flange, strainer
electric, switchboard, switchgear, cable, wiring, power distribution, generator, motor, transformer, circuit, controller

coating, insulation, paint, deck covering, rubber, composite, non-skid, preservation
NAICS-4digit -> bucket crosswalk (fallback when description is thin)
Arbiter principle - subawards classify the buckets (not the total-mix truth)
Subawards map description / vendor / NAICS -> bucket and SEED the observed shares,
but the visible subaward mix is never assumed to equal the hidden TAM mix - the modeled
bucket share is adjusted transparently (SAM -> Subawards), residual shown explicitly.
DDG = DESCRIPTION-led (award description_of_requirement is the primary signal); the submarine
workbook is NAICS/vendor-led. Entity / location are hints only - the award-action scope controls.
Lockheed Aegis, Raytheon SPY-6, BAE Mk45 gun
